title: Scaffold Workflows screen directory structure and screen registry entries
description: Create the `apps/tui/src/screens/Workflows/` directory structure with index barrel exports and register all workflow screen IDs in the screen router registry (`apps/tui/src/router/screens.ts`). Screen IDs to register: `workflow-list`, `workflow-run-list`, `workflow-run-detail`, `workflow-log-viewer`, `workflow-artifacts`, `workflow-caches`. Each screen gets a placeholder component that renders its title and a 'not implemented' message. Wire `g f` go-to keybinding to push `workflow-list` (requires repo context). Wire deep-link `--screen workflows`, `--screen workflow-runs`, `--screen workflow-run`, `--screen workflow-caches` argument parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 workflow screen IDs are registered in the screen registry
`g f` with repo context navigates to workflow-list screen
Deep-link `--screen workflows --repo owner/repo` opens workflow list
Each placeholder screen renders its title and pops on `q`
Breadcrumb trail renders correctly for each screen

title: Implement workflow data hooks adapter for TUI consumption of @codeplane/ui-core
description: Create or wire the data hooks that TUI workflow screens consume from `@codeplane/ui-core`. Hooks needed: `useWorkflowDefinitions(repo, page, perPage)` → `GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/workflows`, `useWorkflowRuns(repo, filters)` → `GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/workflows/runs`, `useWorkflowRunDetail(repo, runId)` → `GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/workflows/runs/:id`, `useWorkflowRunArtifacts(repo, runId)` → `GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/actions/runs/:id/artifacts`, `useWorkflowCaches(repo, filters)` → `GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/caches`, `useWorkflowCacheStats(repo)` → `GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/caches/stats`, `useDispatchWorkflow(repo, workflowId)` → `POST .../dispatches`, `useWorkflowRunCancel(repo, runId)`, `useWorkflowRunRerun(repo, runId)`, `useWorkflowRunResume(repo, runId)`, `useDeleteWorkflowArtifact(repo, runId, name)`, `useDeleteWorkflowCache(repo, cacheId)`. Each hook returns `{ data, loading, error, mutate/loadMore }` consistent with the existing hook API pattern. Mutation hooks support optimistic updates with rollback.
acceptanceCriteria: All workflow data hooks are implemented and export correct TypeScript types
Hooks use the APIClientProvider context for auth and base URL
Mutation hooks implement optimistic update + rollback on error
Page-based pagination hooks support `page` and `per_page` parameters
Loading, error, and empty states are correctly tracked
Memory caps enforced: 300 definitions, 500 runs, 200 artifacts

title: Implement workflow SSE streaming hooks for log streaming and run status events
description: Create SSE hooks for real-time workflow data. `useWorkflowLogStream(owner, repo, runId)` — connects to `GET /api/repos/:owner/:repo/runs/:id/logs` via SSEProvider with ticket-based auth. Returns `{ logs: Map<stepId, LogLine[]>, steps, runStatus, connectionHealth, reconnect, lastEventId }`. Handles event types: `log` (incremental lines), `status` (step/run status changes), `done` (run completion). Deduplicates replayed log lines by log_id. `useWorkflowRunSSE(owner, repo, runIds)` — lighter SSE hook for run list that listens for status transitions on multiple runs. Implements: ticket acquisition via `POST /api/auth/sse-ticket`, reconnection with exponential backoff (1s→30s cap), `Last-Event-ID` header on reconnect, keep-alive timeout at 45s, max 20 reconnection attempts. Connection cleanup on unmount. Virtual scroll window of 10,000 lines per step with FIFO eviction.
acceptanceCriteria: SSE connection established within 500ms of hook mount
Log events render incrementally (no buffer-then-flush)
ANSI color codes in log content preserved for terminal passthrough
Reconnection uses exponential backoff with 30s cap
Last-Event-ID sent on reconnect for event replay
Replayed log lines deduplicated by log_id
Virtual scroll window caps at 10,000 lines per step with FIFO eviction
Connection cleaned up on unmount
connectionHealth exposes 'healthy' | 'degraded' | 'disconnected'

title: Create shared workflow UI utilities: status icons, duration formatting, color mapping
description: Create shared utility module `apps/tui/src/screens/Workflows/utils.ts` with: (1) `getRunStatusIcon(status)` → returns `{ icon: string, color: ThemeToken }` for success(✓/green), failure(✗/red), running(◎/yellow), queued(◌/cyan), cancelled(✕/gray), timeout(⏱/yellow). (2) `getStepStatusIcon(status)` → returns icon/color for success(✓/green), failure(✗/red), running(◎/yellow), pending(◌/gray), skipped(⊘/gray). (3) `formatDuration(seconds | null)` → '45s', '1m 23s', '2h 5m', '—'. (4) `getDurationColor(seconds)` → green(<60), default(60-300), yellow(300-900), red(>900). (5) `formatRelativeTime(timestamp)` → '3d', '1w', '2mo', '1y', 'now', '—'. (6) `getMiniStatusBar(recentRuns[])` → 5-char colored dot string. (7) `formatBytes(bytes)` → '89 B', '345 KB', '2.1 MB', '1.2 GB'. (8) `abbreviateSHA(sha)` → 7-char truncation. (9) `formatRunCount(n)` → K-abbreviation above 999. (10) No-color fallback variants for all icon functions.
acceptanceCriteria: All status icon mappings match the spec (glyph + ANSI color code)
Duration formatting handles null, 0, seconds, minutes, hours correctly
Duration color coding matches spec thresholds
Mini status bar renders exactly 5 chars with correct colors
Byte formatting handles B/KB/MB/GB/TB magnitudes
SHA abbreviation always returns exactly 7 chars
Run count K-abbreviation activates above 999
All functions have no-color fallback variants

title: Implement workflow definition list screen with filtering, search, and mini status bar
description: Implement the Workflow List screen at `apps/tui/src/screens/Workflows/WorkflowListScreen.tsx`. Full-screen view showing all workflow definitions for the current repository. Title row 'Workflows (N)' with total count. Filter toolbar with active/inactive cycling via `f` (All→Active→Inactive→All) and text search via `/`. Scrollable list with WorkflowRow components showing: active status icon (● green / ○ gray), workflow name, file path (truncated from left), latest run status badge, mini status bar (5 colored dots), trigger type, relative timestamp. Vim navigation (j/k/G/gg/Ctrl+D/Ctrl+U). `Enter` pushes workflow-run-list. `d` opens dispatch confirmation for dispatchable workflows. Page-based pagination (page size 30, 300-item cap). Responsive: 80×24 shows icon+name+badge+minibar only; 120×40 adds path, trigger, timestamp; 200×60+ adds run count and avg duration. Status bar hints: 'j/k:nav Enter:runs d:dispatch f:filter q:back'. Write E2E tests in `e2e/tui/workflows.test.ts` covering all SNAP-WF, KEY-WF, RESP-WF, INT-WF, and EDGE-WF test cases (115 tests total).
acceptanceCriteria: Screen renders between header and status bars with correct breadcrumb
Workflow definitions fetched via useWorkflowDefinitions() with page-based pagination
Each row shows: status icon, name, path, latest run badge, mini status bar, trigger, timestamp
Active/inactive filter cycles via `f` with client-side filtering
Text search via `/` filters by name and path
Enter on workflow pushes workflow-run-list with correct context
`d` opens dispatch confirmation for dispatchable workflows, status bar message for non-dispatchable
Responsive layout adapts at all 3 breakpoints
Page-based pagination with 300-item memory cap
Empty state, loading state, and error state render correctly
All 115 E2E tests written and validate spec behavior

title: Implement workflow run list with status filtering, animated spinners, and SSE status updates
description: Implement the Workflow Run List screen at `apps/tui/src/screens/Workflows/WorkflowRunListScreen.tsx`. Shows all runs for a specific workflow definition. Title row '{workflow_name} › Runs (N)'. Filter toolbar with status cycling via `f` (All→Running→Queued→Success→Failure→Cancelled→Finished→All) — status filter triggers server-side API request with `state` param. Text search via `/` for client-side filtering on trigger ref and commit SHA. Run rows show: status icon (animated ◐◓◑◒ spinner for running at 250ms via useTimeline), run ID (#N), trigger event, trigger ref, commit SHA (7-char), duration (color-coded), relative timestamp. Duration color coding: green <1m, default 1-5m, yellow 5-15m, red >15m. Running run elapsed time updates every 1s via local timer. Actions on focused runs: `Enter` opens run detail, `c` cancel, `r` rerun, `m` resume (immediate optimistic, no confirmation overlay). SSE subscription for real-time status transitions on visible running runs. `Ctrl+R` manual refresh. Page-based pagination (page size 30, 500-item memory cap). Responsive at all 3 breakpoints. Write all SNAP-WFR, KEY-WFR, RESP-WFR, INT-WFR, and EDGE-WFR E2E tests (138 total).
acceptanceCriteria: Screen renders with breadcrumb 'Dashboard > owner/repo > Workflows > {name} > Runs'
Run list fetched via useWorkflowRuns() with server-side state filtering
Each row shows: status icon (animated for running), run ID, event, ref, SHA, duration, timestamp
Status filter cycling via `f` triggers fresh API request and resets pagination
Running run spinner animates at 250ms (◐◓◑◒) via useTimeline
Running run elapsed time updates every 1s
Duration color coding matches spec thresholds
Cancel/rerun/resume actions execute immediately with optimistic UI and rollback on error
SSE events update run status inline without manual refresh
`Ctrl+R` forces page re-fetch
Page-based pagination with 500-item memory cap
Responsive layout at all 3 breakpoints
All 138 E2E tests written

title: Implement workflow run detail with step list, inline log expansion, and SSE streaming
description: Implement the Workflow Run Detail screen at `apps/tui/src/screens/Workflows/WorkflowRunDetailScreen.tsx`. Three sections: (1) Metadata header with run status badge (animated for running), workflow name, run #, trigger event/ref, commit SHA, timing info. Elapsed time updates every 1s via useTimeline for active runs. (2) Step list — vertically stacked nodes ordered by position. Each step: status icon, name, duration. `Enter` toggles inline log expansion below step row. `l` opens full-screen log viewer. Steps update in real-time via SSE status events. (3) Inline log panel — when step expanded, logs render below step row with left border, line numbers, ANSI passthrough via `<code>`. SSE log events render incrementally. Auto-follow on by default (toggled with `f`). `e` toggles dispatch inputs section for manually dispatched runs. Confirmation overlays for actions (c/r/R) with spinner during API call. On rerun success, navigates to new run. On resume success, re-establishes SSE. On cancel success, SSE closes. Responsive at all breakpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Screen renders with breadcrumb ending in '#{run-number}'
Run metadata header shows status badge, workflow name, run #, trigger info, timing
Elapsed time updates every 1s for running workflows
Step list renders all nodes ordered by position with status icons
Enter toggles inline log expansion with left-border panel below step
Inline log panel renders lines with line numbers and ANSI passthrough
SSE log events render incrementally in expanded step
SSE status events update step statuses and run status in real-time
Auto-follow scrolls to latest log line (toggleable with `f`)
`l` opens full-screen log viewer for focused step
`e` toggles dispatch inputs section for manually dispatched runs
Action confirmation overlays work with spinner and error handling
Rerun navigates to new run; resume re-establishes SSE; cancel closes SSE
Responsive at all 3 breakpoints

title: Implement full-screen log viewer with SSE streaming, ANSI passthrough, step navigation, and search
description: Implement the full-screen log viewer at `apps/tui/src/screens/Workflows/WorkflowLogViewer.tsx`. Reached via `l` from run detail or deep-link. Layout: step selector bar at top with status badges per step, log scrollbox below. Step selector: `[`/`]` for prev/next step, number keys 1-9 for direct selection. Each step has independent scroll position. Log content: line numbers in gutter (4ch at 80×24, 6ch at 120×40, 8ch at 200×60+), stream indicator column (stdout/stderr, hidden at 80×24 where stderr uses red line number), and log content with ANSI color passthrough. SSE streaming via useWorkflowLogStream: incremental rendering, ticket-based auth, exponential backoff reconnection, Last-Event-ID replay, dedup by log_id. Auto-follow on by default for in-progress runs; toggled via `f`; disabled on manual scroll; re-enabled via `G`. Step badges update via SSE status events (spinner for running at 80ms via useTimeline). Search via `/`: opens input, highlights matches in reverse video, `n`/`N` navigate matches, operates on ANSI-stripped text, case-insensitive. Connection health indicator in status bar (green/yellow/red dot). `R` manual reconnect (debounced 2s). Virtual scrolling for 100K+ lines with 10K-line window. At 80×24: collapsed step selector `[< build >]`. At 200×60+: step durations and byte count. Write all LOG-* E2E tests (97 total).
acceptanceCriteria: SSE connection established within 500ms of mount with ticket-based auth
Log lines render incrementally within 16ms of SSE event receipt
ANSI color codes in log content are passed through to terminal
Line numbers rendered in muted gutter, stderr distinguished by color/indicator
Step selector bar shows all steps with status badges that update via SSE
`[`/`]` navigate steps with independent scroll positions preserved
Auto-follow enabled by default; disabled on manual scroll; toggled with `f`; re-enabled with `G`
Search (`/`) highlights matches, `n`/`N` navigate, operates on ANSI-stripped text
Reconnection with exponential backoff + Last-Event-ID replay + dedup
Connection health shown in status bar as colored dot
Virtual scrolling supports 100K+ lines with 10K window
Responsive at all 3 breakpoints with step selector adaptation
All 97 LOG-* E2E tests written

title: Implement cancel/rerun/resume action system with confirmation overlays and optimistic updates
description: Implement the cross-cutting workflow action system that serves both the run detail and run list screens. Two interaction models: (1) On run detail: `c` cancel (running/queued), `r` rerun (terminal states), `R` resume (cancelled/failed) — each opens a confirmation overlay with action label, run #, workflow name, Confirm/Cancel buttons. Spinner during API call. Error displayed inline in overlay with retry option. On success: cancel→status transitions to cancelled + SSE closes; rerun→navigate to new run; resume→SSE re-establishes. (2) On run list: `c` cancel, `r` rerun, `m` resume — immediate optimistic update without confirmation overlay. Optimistic state reverts with 200ms red flash on API error. State gating: pressing action key in incompatible state shows 3s auto-dismiss status bar message. Permission gating: read-only users see dimmed hints, get 'Permission denied' on action. Handle 403 (permission denied), 409 (state conflict), 404 (not found), 429 (rate limit). Responsive confirmation overlays: 90% at 80×24, 40% at 120×40, 35% at 200×60+. SSE status event during open overlay updates state; if incompatible, overlay shows 'Run state changed' and dismisses.
acceptanceCriteria: Cancel/rerun/resume available from both run detail (with confirmation) and run list (optimistic)
Actions call correct API endpoints: POST .../cancel, .../rerun, .../resume
State gating enforced: cancel only running/queued, rerun only terminal, resume only cancelled/failed
Invalid state press shows descriptive 3s status bar message
Permission gating: dimmed hints for read-only, 'Permission denied' on action
Confirmation overlay shows action label, run #, workflow name, Confirm/Cancel buttons
Spinner during API call replaces Confirm button text
Error handling: 403, 409, 404, 429 responses handled with user-visible messages
On rerun success, new run ID returned and navigated to on detail screen
Optimistic updates on run list revert with 200ms red flash on error
SSE status event during open overlay triggers state reconciliation

title: Implement workflow dispatch modal with dynamic form, ref selector, and input type resolution
description: Implement the workflow dispatch overlay at `apps/tui/src/screens/Workflows/components/DispatchOverlay.tsx`. Triggered by `d` on a dispatchable workflow in the workflow list. Centered modal with border in primary color, surface background. Header: 'Dispatch Workflow' bold primary + workflow name. Ref input pre-populated with 'main'. Dynamic form fields from workflow's `on.workflow_dispatch.inputs` config: string inputs → `<input>`, boolean inputs → `[true]`/`[false]` toggle (Space/Enter), choice inputs → `<select>` dropdown (j/k navigate, Enter select). Input fields show label (key name), pre-filled default value, and muted description text. Tab order: Ref → inputs → Dispatch → Cancel. `Ctrl+S` or Enter on Dispatch submits. `Esc` or Enter on Cancel dismisses. During submission: 'Dispatching...' + spinner, fields non-interactive, double-submit prevention. On 204 success: overlay closes, status bar flashes 'Workflow dispatched ✓' green 3s, run summaries refresh. Error responses (400/403/404/409/429/network) show inline red error. On 401: overlay closes → auth error screen. Ref validation: no `..`, no control chars, no leading/trailing slashes, max 255 chars. Input values max 1000 chars. Max 20 rendered inputs. `d` on non-dispatchable: status bar 'Workflow does not support manual dispatch'. `d` without write access: status bar 'Permission denied'. Responsive: 90% at 80×24, 50% at standard/large. Focus trap in overlay. Write all SNAP-WFD, KEY-WFD, RESP-WFD, INT-WFD, EDGE-WFD tests (76 total).
acceptanceCriteria: `d` on dispatchable workflow opens centered dispatch modal overlay
`d` on non-dispatchable shows status bar message, no overlay
`d` without write access shows 'Permission denied'
Ref input pre-populated with 'main', editable, validates against patterns
Dynamic form fields render based on workflow config input types
Boolean inputs toggle between [true]/[false] on Space/Enter
Choice inputs render as select dropdown with j/k/Enter interaction
Tab/Shift+Tab navigates form fields in correct order
Ctrl+S submits from any field; Enter on Dispatch button submits
During submission: spinner, disabled fields, double-submit prevention
Success: overlay closes, status bar flash, run summaries refresh
Error responses handled inline within overlay
Focus trapped within overlay; global keys suppressed
Responsive sizing at all breakpoints
All 76 dispatch E2E tests written

title: Implement artifact browser with detail overlay, download delegation, and delete confirmation
description: Implement the Workflow Artifacts View at `apps/tui/src/screens/Workflows/WorkflowArtifactsView.tsx`. Full-screen view of artifacts for a specific workflow run, reached via `a` from run detail. Title row 'Artifacts (N)' with total combined size. Filter toolbar with status cycling (All→Ready→Pending→Expired) via `f`, sort cycling (created/name/size in asc/desc) via `s`, and text search via `/`. Artifact rows: status icon (● ready/green, ◎ pending/yellow, ○ expired/gray), name, content type, size (human-readable via formatBytes), expiration countdown, release indicator (📎), created timestamp. `Enter` opens detail overlay (60%×50% modal with full metadata: name, type, size in raw+formatted, status, created/confirmed/expires timestamps, GCS path truncated, release attachment details). `D` (shift+d) initiates download via CLI delegation (`codeplane artifact download`), shows progress in status bar. `x` opens delete confirmation overlay (40%×25%, error border, 'Are you sure?' prompt). Delete: optimistic removal, `DELETE /api/repos/:owner/:repo/actions/runs/:id/artifacts/:name`. No pagination (all loaded at once, 200-item cap). Responsive at all breakpoints. Write all SNAP-ART, KEY-ART, RESP-ART, INT-ART, EDGE-ART tests (122 total).
acceptanceCriteria: Screen reachable via `a` from run detail, shows correct breadcrumb
Title shows 'Artifacts (N)' with total combined size
Artifact rows show: status icon, name, content type, size, expiration, release indicator, timestamp
Status filter (f) and sort (s) cycling work with client-side filtering
Enter opens detail overlay with full metadata including release attachment info
D initiates download via CLI delegation with status bar progress
x opens delete confirmation; Enter confirms; Esc cancels
Delete is optimistic with rollback on error
Pending/expired artifacts block download with appropriate messages
Responsive at all breakpoints (columns adapt)
Empty state, loading state, error state render correctly
All 122 artifact E2E tests written

title: Implement cache management screen with statistics banner, filters, single/bulk delete
description: Implement the Workflow Cache View at `apps/tui/src/screens/Workflows/WorkflowCacheView.tsx`. Repository-level screen reached via `a` from workflow list, `:caches` command palette, or `--screen workflow-caches` deep-link. Layout: (1) Statistics banner showing cache count, total storage (formatBytes), repo quota with visual usage bar (Unicode block chars ████░░ with percentage), max archive size, TTL, last hit timestamp. Usage bar color: default <90%, yellow ≥90%, red =100%. (2) Filter bar with bookmark filter (`b`), key filter (`f`), text search (`/`), clear all filters (`x`), sort indicator. Active filters shown as pills. (3) Scrollable cache list with rows: status icon (✓ finalized/green, ◌ pending/yellow), cache key, bookmark name, size, hit count, last hit, expiration. `Enter` expands inline detail. `d` single-cache delete with confirmation overlay. `D` bulk-clear all matching filters with confirmation showing count/size. `s` sort cycling (created/last hit/size/hits). `R` manual refresh. Page-based pagination (30 per page, 500 total cap). Responsive at all breakpoints (stats banner adapts from 1 to 3 lines).
acceptanceCriteria: Screen reachable via `a` from workflow list, `:caches` command palette, and deep-link
Statistics banner shows cache count, storage used/quota, usage bar with percentage, TTL, last hit
Usage bar renders with correct colors based on usage percentage
Filter bar supports bookmark filter (`b`), key filter (`f`), search (`/`), clear all (`x`)
Active filters displayed as pills with clear individual/all options
Cache rows show: status icon, key, bookmark, size, hits, last hit, expiration
Enter expands inline detail for focused cache entry
`d` single-cache delete with confirmation overlay
`D` bulk-clear with confirmation showing count and total size of matching caches
`s` cycles sort order (created/last hit/size/hits)
Pagination with 30 per page, 500 total cap
Responsive: stats banner adapts from 1 line (80×24) to 3 lines (200×60+)
Empty state, loading state, error state render correctly
